behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model behavior (speaker diarization/label preservation) on real audio; a stubbed transcript would hide mislabeled speakers. Rejected Workflow: behavior targets one model judgment boundary (STT label fidelity), not a chained multi-agent decision sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the real STT API and assert speaker labels are preserved in the transcript fields consumed by summarization (no dropped/swapped attendee labels).

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is parser/transform logic on transcript text and output schema; the failure is local extraction logic, not inherently OS/network/model dependent. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff to surface the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript lines into the parsing function and assert extracted assignment objects contain correct assignee and task text in the summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output content (coverage of assignees in generated action items). Deterministic fixtures would mask misses in model reasoning. Rejected Workflow: can be validated with a single summarization model decision from a prepared transcript.
behaviors.test_description: Use a transcript with multiple explicit assignees, call the real summarization LLM, and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one produced action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly about routing already-identified decisions into the correct schema field; that is local mapping logic. Rejected Integration: no explicit external boundary or module handoff is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision items, execute routing/mapping logic and assert output places them only under `decisions` and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the real LLM surfaces and classifies decisions correctly in generated output; stubs would always pass placement expectations. Rejected Workflow: single summarization-step model judgment is sufficient to expose this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Send a transcript containing clear decision statements to the real summarization LLM and assert those decisions appear in the structured `decisions` section rather than only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-stage processing across segments plus coherent merged output; this is multiple agentic steps (repeated model calls and final merged summary quality) with ordering/state carryover. Lower levels miss cross-step degradation.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation flow, verify sequential segment processing and merged final output, and evaluate that merged summary preserves cross-segment action items/decisions coherently.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end orchestration with real recording and chained model-dependent stages (transcription then summarization then delivery). It is inherently multi-step agentic pipeline validation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting file in the deployed pipeline: upload, STT, LLM summary generation, and structured output delivery; assert completion and valid populated schema without manual fixes.
